hypothesis: If catalytic surface compositions are generated via a conditional latent diffusion model in a chemically informed autoencoder latent space conditioned on adsorbate identity, target adsorption energy, and allowed elements, then the resulting 2D shell-wise compositions will better match ground-truth distributions and instances (lower Fréchet distance, higher cosine similarity) with fewer constraint violations (lower leakage) than a chemically-agnostic baseline, including under limited data, because the chemical prior and conditioning embed physically relevant constraints that guide inverse design.
